hub_subtitle: Business English from the ground up -- your work, your team, your routine

lesson.menu_desc: Can / can't for ability: talk about what you can do at work -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Skills & Abilities -- What You Can Do at Work
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danilo Febba | Aula 9 -- Skills & Abilities

listenings.text: Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. Quick update about the new project team. Everyone can do different things. Sofia can code in Python very well, and she can build the data pipeline. Marcus can design the dashboards, but he can't write the database queries yet, so he needs a little help. I can plan the schedule, and I can present our results to the client. You can troubleshoot the system fast, and you can handle big datasets, so you are very important for this team. Can you start on Monday? Let me know. Thanks. Bye!

Let me tell you what I can do at work. I am a data engineer, so I can code in Python, and I can build data pipelines every week. I can design simple dashboards, and I can operate our main system. I can handle large datasets, and when something breaks, I can troubleshoot the problem and fix it fast. I can't design beautiful graphics. That is not my ability. But I can present my results in a clear way. Every month, I learn one new thing that I can do.
